claimText: FASTag employs Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology to enable toll payments directly from a linked account while the vehicle is in motion.
evidenceExcerpt: FASTAG - A device that employs Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for making toll payments directly from account while the vehicle is in motion.
examUse: Statement-based question on the underlying technology of FASTag.

claimText: RFID is a short-range technology that utilizes radio waves to communicate information between tags and nearby readers.
evidenceExcerpt: RFID consists of tags and readers and employs radio waves to communicate information of objects or people to nearby readers. - It is a short-range technology.
examUse: Statement-based question on the characteristics and working principle of RFID.

claimText: FASTag was developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) under the National Electronic Toll Collection (NETC) program.
evidenceExcerpt: Developed by: NPCI under National Electronic Toll Collection (NETC) program.
examUse: Institution-feature question identifying the developing agency of FASTag.
distractorHooks: Centre for Development of Advanced Computing (C-DAC)
